goal:
Implement job chaining for the orchestrator. This allows a supervisor to submit multiple goals that execute sequentially - when job N completes successfully, job N+1 starts automatically.

Changes needed:

1. In internal/domain/types.go, add a JobChain struct with fields: ID string, Goals []ChainGoal, CurrentIndex int, Status string (running/done/failed), CreatedAt/UpdatedAt time.Time. ChainGoal has: Goal string, Provider ProviderName, StrictnessLevel string, ContextMode string, MaxSteps int, JobID string (filled when started), Status string (pending/running/done/failed).

2. In internal/orchestrator/service.go, add methods:
- StartChain(ctx, goals []ChainGoal, workspaceDir string) (*JobChain, error) -- creates chain, starts first job
- advanceChain(ctx, chain *JobChain) -- called when a job completes, starts next goal or marks chain done
- GetChain(ctx, chainID string) (*JobChain, error)
- ListChains(ctx) ([]*JobChain, error)
Wire advanceChain into the job completion path (after job reaches "done" status, check if it belongs to a chain and advance).

3. In internal/mcp/server.go, add MCP tools:
- gorechera_start_chain: params goals (array of objects with goal, provider, strictness_level, context_mode, max_steps), workspace_dir. Returns chain ID.
- gorechera_chain_status: params chain_id. Returns chain state with all goals and their job IDs/statuses.

4. In internal/store/state_store.go, add SaveChain/LoadChain/ListChains for file-based persistence (same pattern as jobs).

Run go build ./... and go test ./... to verify.
